action.reasoning: Observation: All heuristic controllers yield safe_follow near zero (avg 1.3 across 6 eps). Train test shows robot collides and rarely achieves simultaneous distance/angle constraint. Navmesh pathfinder previous attempts may have returned empty waypoints, but we haven't verified with a standalone test. Analysis: Without global navigation, reactive obstacle avoidance is unable to reach moving human. Assume pathfinder may be functional but was misused; if so, waypoint following could finally work. Decision: Run a quick diagnostic test of navmesh pathfinder to see if waypoints can be generated. If yes, implement a waypoint-based controller in train.py/predict.py; if no, pivot to RL with depth input for obstacle avoidance.
